note: Evidence partitions = the disjoint 'views' granted to assessors. The primary diversity lever: an assessor may only use what its partition grants. `sources`/`claims` are A1 fixture slugs (fixtures/INDEX.json). show_excerpts=false means the assessor sees the source's bibliographic metadata and the claim texts but NOT the abstract text — so it cannot independently verify grounding and must rely on unverified summaries. An assessor asked a probe about evidence outside its partition should answer UNCERTAIN; that honest abstention is part of what decorrelates the panel.

FULL.desc: Sees everything (both abstracts + all four extracted claims). Highest information, but shared with every other FULL assessor — the correlated baseline.

WOROBEY_ONLY.desc: Only the Worobey market-proximity evidence; blind to the Pekar molecular line.

PEKAR_ONLY.desc: Only the Pekar molecular-epidemiology evidence; blind to the Worobey proximity lines.

PROXIMITY_SPATIAL.desc: Only the two spatial proximity lines; blind to live-mammal trade and molecular evidence.

PROXIMITY_TRADE.desc: Only the trade-and-molecular lines; blind to the geographic/environmental spatial evidence.

SOURCES_ONLY.desc: Both raw abstracts, no pre-extracted claims — must read and interpret the sources unaided.

CLAIMS_ONLY.desc: The four claim texts + source citations but NOT the abstracts — relies on unverified summaries, cannot check grounding.
